title: Early satiety documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.
assessment.rationale: The exact adult dyspepsia section supports meal-related upper-GI symptoms, nausea, bloating, pain, duration, triggers, and impact without diagnosing a motility disorder.

source.title: Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease and dyspepsia in adults: investigation and management
source.population: Adults aged 18 years and over with dyspepsia, symptoms suggestive of gastro-oesophageal reflux disease, or both.
source.setting: Community, primary-care, investigation, review, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult reflux and dyspepsia symptom, alarm, investigation, review, and clinician-discussed management fields; the legacy workflow has no adult-only filter and UAE pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Scope and recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.2 and 1.4.1 — symptom profile and common care

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

assessment.text: Supports clinician recognition of dysphagia, weight-loss combinations, and jaundice in recommendation-specific age and symptom contexts; it does not assert cancer or an automatic referral decision.
assessment.rationale: The exact upper-GI cancer section supports clinician-assessed weight loss, dysphagia, jaundice, and concern context without asserting disease.

source.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with symptoms or signs that may indicate cancer, subject to recommendation-specific age criteria.
source.setting: Primary care recognition, safety netting, investigation, and referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected suspicious skin-lesion, safety-netting, and age- or risk-qualified respiratory recommendations; international suspected-cancer pathways require UAE clinician review. This batch also reviews recommendation-qualified upper-GI dysphagia, jaundice, and colorectal rectal-bleeding fields; no cancer diagnosis or automatic referral text is generated. assessment.rationale: The exact section supports documenting existing results and clinician-entered review without ordering or interpreting tests.
